segs.utf8:
-Welcome back to the show.
-[ Laughs ]

-I'm so happy for you.
The album is out.

We can say it.
It's officially out right now.

You can -- You can download it.
[ Cheers and applause ]

-Wow.
-"Short n' Sweet,"

I mean, come on, look at that,
with the colored vinyl, too.

[ Cheers and applause ]
-Yeah. Wow.

The whole presentation.
-It's fantastic.

-There's an insert.
There's also there's a poster.
-The poster in there.

-There's all sorts of things
in here.

-Congrats on this.
Man, this is your summer.

-Thank you.
This is the summer

of Sabrina Carpenter, come on.
-I'm so happy to be here.

Thank you.
[ Cheers and applause ]
-We love you.

I have a few things
I want to read to you here.

"Espresso" has been
certified platinum,

and it was one
of the fastest songs

to reach a billion streams
in the history of Spotify.

[ Cheers and applause ]
In the history.

Number two,
"Please Please Please"

went to number one
on the global Spotify chart,

dethroning you,
who already had

the number-one spot
with "Espresso"!

-[ Chuckles ]
[ Cheers and applause ]

-And also, get ready for this.
I don't know if you know this.

-I don't know this.
-You became the first artist

since the Beatles
to have two songs debut

within the top three
on the Billboard Hot 100.

Sabrina Carpenter.
[ Cheers and applause ]

That's what I'm talking about.
Come on.

That's what I'm talking about.
-That one sounds fake to me.

-No, they're all real.
-The Beatles one sounds fake.

-Yeah.
No, the Beatles one is real.

-But there's one of me
and four of them,

so I don't understand the math.
-How cool is that

to be in the same sentence
as the Beatles?

-Very cool.
That's an understatement.

I don't really have
the words for it.

I'm just so grateful
that you guys are listening.

And I genuinely was, like,
thinking about all the places

that would be
so incredible to be

on the night
of the album release,
because it means so much to me.

And when they told me
I was going to be here,

I was like, "
Absolutely perfect.

There's nowhere else
I'd rather be."

So, I just love you so much.
-I'm so happy!

Congratulations!
-Thank you!

-Congratulations. I'm so happy.
-Thanks.

-Alright, your songs
have been everywhere,

the past few months.
People can't stop singing them.

But there was a video
of Adele talking about

how "Espresso" was the last
thing she thought about

before she went to bed.
We have a clip. Watch this.
-I got into bed last night,

because it was just
a very late night for me --

I'm normally in bed
by 9:00 p.m. --
I found myself singing...

♪ I'm working late
'cause I'm a singer ♪

That Sabrina Carpenter song.
That song is my jam!

-"That song is my jam!
That song is my jam!"

I love that.
-"That Sabrina Carpenter song."

-Like, I just love how I'm like,
"Does she have social media?

Like, how does she know
about the song or anything?"

And that was the most
mind-blowing thing to me.

-It's Adele.
-It's Adele.

--It's Adele.
-One name. That's it.

-Exactly, one name. That's it.
-Yeah. So cool.

-The Internet noticed
that you seem to be able

to manifest things,
by the way, on social media.

I don't know
if you know about this,

but here's an example
of something --
something that you tweeted.

It said, "People are always
scared to give me caffeine.

I don't know why.
Those little espresso things

taste cute and I like them.
-[ Snickers ]

[ Laughter and cheers ]
-What's the date?

-I mean...
-March 16, 2016.

[ Crowd cheering ]
-I mean, espresso.

-So, I was 15,
drinking espresso.

[ Laughter ]
Which says a lot.

-Yeah, it does say a lot,
right there, yeah.

-About my parents or me,
I don't know.

-I think you can
predict the future,

so be careful now,
when you tweet things out,

because you become real.
-What do I tweet tonight?

Let me tweet from your phone.
Where's your phone?

What do we tweet?
What do we tweet?

-That you're going to have
the number-one album.

-Ohh!
-That's what I'm talking about.

-Oh! [ Laughs ]
-"Short 'n Sweet."

That's what I'm talking about.
Short n' Sweet" will be

the number-one album.
You watch, you watch.

"Please Please Please,"
go back to number one.

All of this stuff's
going to happen.

It officially came out
at midnight.

Do you have any favorite songs
on here?

-No.
-You don't?

[ Laughter ]
-Um...

-I like "Taste," I like "Taste."
That's the next one.

-Wait, you heard it?
-Of course.

You gave me the digital.
I had to sign all these

passwords and passcodes.
-I'm sorry.

-I had to go into a special room
and put headphones on.

-With padding.
-Yeah, I did. It's amazing.

-That's so cool
that you heard it.

-I can say now,
"Taste" is track one.

-Yeah.
-That's also the secret video.

-Yeah, well,
it's not a secret anymore,

but it's coming out tomorrow.
-Yes.

-I'm so excited about --
-Still half the secret.

-Yeah.
-So kind of a secret.

-It's kind of a secret.
I mean, honestly, like,

it does really feel like
a self-titled album to me,

in a weird --
Well, also 'cause

"Short n' Sweet"
is sort of like "Sabrina"

in another language to me,
and it really just feels

like all of these --
these stories and memories,

and very much, like, blunt,
very forward, very fun,

and just some
of the best memories I've had,

over the last two years
of writing it.

So I'm so excited
for everyone to hear it.

-It's really great.
-Thank you.

-Yeah, I...
[ Cheers and applause ]

-[ Laughs ]
They're great.

-They're the best.
-They're the best.

-One of the things
we talked about

is that you like
to hide Easter eggs

and do little things
for the video for "Taste,"

that's dropping tomorrow.
This is what you posted.

And everyone's like, "Who is it?
Who is it, who is it?"

-Correct.
-And then we kind of

maybe know who it is.
-Yeah, she was just here.

I was like, "I just missed you."
-Janet Ortega.

-Janet Ortega, yeah!
[ Cheers and applause ]

-I know. It's fantastic.
But I love that you did that.

-Yeah, because, I mean,
one of -- like,

it was inspired by one
of my favorite films,

and she was a huge fan
of the film.

And also, just, like,
I'm so excited

for you guys to see this video.
You have no idea.

I think it's my favorite one
I've ever done.

-Really?
-So, yeah.

[ Cheers and applause ]
-You're also about to kick off

your big headlining tour
next month.

You're everywhere from
Madison Square Garden

to The O2 -- The O2 in London.
[ Cheers and applause ]

How are you approaching
putting that show together?

-Um, you know...
[ Laughs ]

I don't know.
It's my first-ever arena tour,

and I've only ever
seen shows there.

And I'm so small,
and those stage so big.

So I don't really know
what I will do,

other than I want it to be
the best show

for everybody that's attending.
[ Cheers and applause ]

And...
-[ Chuckles ]

-I'm so excited.
-And The Roots

are going to be there.
-Roots will be there! Come on.

-[ Laughs ]
-We're all invited!

Let's go to London!
-Everyone's invited.
[ Cheers and applause ]

-You're going to perform
"Please Please Please" tonight.

We cannot wait for that.
Sabrina Carpenter,

Short n' Sweet"
is officially out now.

We're celebrating,
and she's performing for us

later in the show.
More "Tonight Show"

after the break!
Stick around everybody!

Sabrina Carpenter!
[ Cheers and applause ]
